# UC-22.2.23 · NIS2 Incident Handling Procedure Adherence and Playbook Execution

## Description

Policies on handling incidents must be effective in practice. This use case tracks whether incident tickets traverse required playbook stages (triage, containment, eradication, recovery, lessons learned) within SLA — correlating SOC workflow fields with NIS2 incident classes.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:incident. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Standardise work-note keywords for each playbook stage; (2) link ES notables to ServiceNow `correlation_id`; (3) alert IR leads on missing stages before closure; (4) include lessons-learned completion in post-incident review; (5) export metrics for supervisory evidence packs. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:incident`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `` `notable` `` macro, `index=itsm` linked incidents, `nis2_playbook_stage_map.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Standardise work-note keywords for each playbook stage; (2) link ES notables to ServiceNow `correlation_id`; (3) alert IR leads on missing stages before closure; (4) include lessons-learned completion in post-incident review; (5) export metrics for supervisory evidence packs.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") earliest=-30d
| eval incident_id=rule_name."|"._time
| join incident_id type=left [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-30d
    | eval incident_id=correlation_id
    | eval stages_complete=mvcount(mvappend(
        if(match(work_notes,"(?i)contain"),"contain",""),
        if(match(work_notes,"(?i)erad"),"erad",""),
        if(match(work_notes,"(?i)recover"),"recover",""),
        if(match(work_notes,"(?i)lessons"),"lessons","")))
    | stats latest(stages_complete) as stages_complete by incident_id
]
| eval playbook_gap=if(stages_complete < 4, 1, 0)
| where playbook_gap=1 AND status!="Closed"
| table _time, rule_name, status, owner, stages_complete
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Incident Handling Procedure Adherence and Playbook Execution (Art. 21(2)(b))** — Policies on handling incidents must be effective in practice. This use case tracks whether incident tickets traverse required playbook stages (triage, containment, eradication, recovery, lessons learned) within SLA — correlating SOC workflow fields with NIS2 incident classes.

Documented **Data sources**: `` `notable` `` macro, `index=itsm` linked incidents, `nis2_playbook_stage_map.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Invokes macro `notable` — in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **incident_id** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **playbook_gap** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where playbook_gap=1 AND status!="Closed"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, incident_id, owner, playbook_gap, rule_name, stages_complete, status.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare sample results with the ES Incident Review queue and analyst outcomes so counts, severities, and fields match what your security operations team triages in production.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Funnel chart (stage completion), Table (incomplete incidents), Single value (% full playbook adherence).

## SPL

```spl
`notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") earliest=-30d
| eval incident_id=rule_name."|"._time
| join incident_id type=left [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-30d
    | eval incident_id=correlation_id
    | eval stages_complete=mvcount(mvappend(
        if(match(work_notes,"(?i)contain"),"contain",""),
        if(match(work_notes,"(?i)erad"),"erad",""),
        if(match(work_notes,"(?i)recover"),"recover",""),
        if(match(work_notes,"(?i)lessons"),"lessons","")))
    | stats latest(stages_complete) as stages_complete by incident_id
]
| eval playbook_gap=if(stages_complete < 4, 1, 0)
| where playbook_gap=1 AND status!="Closed"
| table _time, rule_name, status, owner, stages_complete
```

## Visualization

Funnel chart (stage completion), Table (incomplete incidents), Single value (% full playbook adherence). Key fields: `incident_id`, `owner`, `playbook_gap`, `rule_name`, `stages_complete`, `status`.
